lines.english: Lesson 10
lines.chinese: 第10课
lines.raw: Lesson 10 | 第10课

lines.english: The loss of the Titanic
lines.chinese: 泰坦尼克号的沉没
lines.raw: The loss of the Titanic | 泰坦尼克号的沉没

lines.english: What'd have happened if only two of the 16 water-tight compartments had been flooded?
lines.chinese: 如果16个水密舱室中只有两个被淹没，会发生什么？
lines.raw: What'd have happened if only two of the 16 water-tight compartments had been flooded? | 如果16个水密舱室中只有两个被淹没，会发生什么？

lines.english: The great ship, Titanic sailed for New York from Southampton on April 10th, 1912.
lines.chinese: 1912年4月10日，巨轮“泰坦尼克号”从南安普顿启程驶往纽约。
lines.raw: The great ship, Titanic sailed for New York from Southampton on April 10th, 1912. | 1912年4月10日，巨轮“泰坦尼克号”从南安普顿启程驶往纽约。

lines.english: She was carrying 1, 316 passengers and a crew of 891.
lines.chinese: 船上载有1316名乘客和891名船员。
lines.raw: She was carrying 1, 316 passengers and a crew of 891. | 船上载有1316名乘客和891名船员。

lines.english: Even by modern standards, the 46, 000 ton Titanic was a colossal ship.
lines.chinese: 即使按现代标准来看，46000吨的泰坦尼克号也是一艘巨轮。
lines.raw: Even by modern standards, the 46, 000 ton Titanic was a colossal ship. | 即使按现代标准来看，46000吨的泰坦尼克号也是一艘巨轮。

lines.english: At that time, however, she was not only the largest ship that had ever been built, but was regarded as unsinkable, for she had sixteen water-tight compartments.
lines.chinese: 然而，在那个时代，她不仅是当时建造过的最大的船只，而且被认为是永不沉没的，因为她有十六个水密舱室。
lines.raw: At that time, however, she was not only the largest ship that had ever been built, but was regarded as unsinkable, for she had sixteen water-tight compartments. | 然而，在那个时代，她不仅是当时建造过的最大的船只，而且被认为是永不沉没的，因为她有十六个水密舱室。

lines.english: Even if two of these were flooded, she would still be able to float.
lines.chinese: 即使其中两个舱室被水淹没，她仍然能够漂浮。
lines.raw: Even if two of these were flooded, she would still be able to float. | 即使其中两个舱室被水淹没，她仍然能够漂浮。

lines.english: The tragic sinking of this great liner will always be remembered, for she went down on her first voyage with heavy loss of life.
lines.chinese: 这艘巨型邮轮的悲剧性沉没将永远被铭记，因为她在首次航行中就沉没了，造成了大量人员伤亡。
lines.raw: The tragic sinking of this great liner will always be remembered, for she went down on her first voyage with heavy loss of life. | 这艘巨型邮轮的悲剧性沉没将永远被铭记，因为她在首次航行中就沉没了，造成了大量人员伤亡。

lines.english: Four days after setting out, while the Titanic was sailing across the icy waters of the North Atlantic,
lines.chinese: 启程四天后，当泰坦尼克号航行在北大西洋冰冷的海域时，
lines.raw: Four days after setting out, while the Titanic was sailing across the icy waters of the North Atlantic, | 启程四天后，当泰坦尼克号航行在北大西洋冰冷的海域时，

lines.english: a huge iceberg was suddenly spotted by a lookout.
lines.chinese: 一名瞭望员突然发现了一座巨大的冰山。
lines.raw: a huge iceberg was suddenly spotted by a lookout. | 一名瞭望员突然发现了一座巨大的冰山。

lines.english: After the alarm had been given, the great ship turned sharply to avoid a direct collision.
lines.chinese: 警报发出后，这艘巨轮急剧转向，以避免直接碰撞。
lines.raw: After the alarm had been given, the great ship turned sharply to avoid a direct collision. | 警报发出后，这艘巨轮急剧转向，以避免直接碰撞。

lines.english: The Titanic turned just in time, narrowly missing the immense wall of ice which rose over 100 feet out of the water beside her.
lines.chinese: 泰坦尼克号及时转向，险些错过了她旁边那堵高出水面100多英尺的巨大冰墙。
lines.raw: The Titanic turned just in time, narrowly missing the immense wall of ice which rose over 100 feet out of the water beside her. | 泰坦尼克号及时转向，险些错过了她旁边那堵高出水面100多英尺的巨大冰墙。

lines.english: Suddenly, there was a slight trembling sound from below,
lines.chinese: 突然，从下方传来一阵轻微的颤抖声，
lines.raw: Suddenly, there was a slight trembling sound from below, | 突然，从下方传来一阵轻微的颤抖声，

lines.english: and the captain went down to see what had happened.
lines.chinese: 船长下去查看发生了什么。
lines.raw: and the captain went down to see what had happened. | 船长下去查看发生了什么。

lines.english: The noise had been so faint that no one thought that the ship had been damaged.
lines.chinese: 声音如此微弱，以至于没有人认为船只受到了损坏。
lines.raw: The noise had been so faint that no one thought that the ship had been damaged. | 声音如此微弱，以至于没有人认为船只受到了损坏。

lines.english: Below, the captain realized to his horror that the Titanic was sinking rapidly,
lines.chinese: 在下方，船长惊恐地意识到泰坦尼克号正在迅速下沉，
lines.raw: Below, the captain realized to his horror that the Titanic was sinking rapidly, | 在下方，船长惊恐地意识到泰坦尼克号正在迅速下沉，

lines.english: for five of her sixteen watertight compartments had already been flooded!
lines.chinese: 因为她16个水密舱室中的5个已经进水了！
lines.raw: for five of her sixteen watertight compartments had already been flooded! | 因为她16个水密舱室中的5个已经进水了！

lines.english: The order to abandon ship was given and hundreds of people plunged into the icy water.
lines.chinese: 弃船命令下达，数百人跳入冰冷的水中。
lines.raw: The order to abandon ship was given and hundreds of people plunged into the icy water. | 弃船命令下达，数百人跳入冰冷的水中。

lines.english: As there were not enough life-boats for everybody, 1, 500 lives were lost.
lines.chinese: 由于救生艇不足，有1500人丧生。
lines.raw: As there were not enough life-boats for everybody, 1, 500 lives were lost. | 由于救生艇不足，有1500人丧生。
